title: Contract Scope
description: A line in a customer's commercial scope of work, sourced from a contract sheet (DELTA ELCOM 'SCS' tab or equivalent). Carries the structured commercial information: which job plans are in scope, the asset count covered, per-cycle costs (cycle_costs JSONB), per-year base totals (year_totals JSONB, BEFORE CPI), and when assets fall due (due_years JSONB). Audit flags has_bundled_scope and commercial_gap surface walkthrough-discovered anomalies (e.g. T&T delivered inside a fixed JP price, or non-zero labour with zero annual fee).

customer_id.description: Customer this scope row belongs to.

site_id.description: Site this scope row covers. Null when the scope applies to all customer sites.

financial_year.description: Australian FY this scope row applies to (e.g. '2025-2026').

scope_item.description: Free-text description of the scope line (preserved from legacy rows). New imports also populate jp_code + structured fields.

is_included.description: Whether this line is included in scope (true) or explicitly excluded (false).

billing_basis.description: Billing model. 'fixed' = priced as part of the contract (JPs, priced Additional Items). 'ad_hoc' = billed per visit, no fixed annual price (e.g. RCD/T&T at SY9).

jp_code.description: Job plan code (mirror of maintenance_plan.code, denormalised for CSV import speed). Used to resolve the maintenance_plan FK at commit time.

asset_qty.description: Count of assets at this site / customer covered by this scope row.

intervals_text.description: Raw free-text intervals as they appear in the source sheet (e.g. 'A; 5', 'M/Q/A', 'Q/A'). Normalisation happens later.

cycle_costs.description: Per-asset cost at each cycle frequency, read from the JP tab 'Total cost per asset' row. NEVER reconstruct from labour x rate — each JP tab has its own per-frequency formula. Shape: { '1YR': 31.25, '5YR': 562.50, ... }. Keys: 1YR, 2YR, 3YR, 4YR, 5YR, 8YR, 10YR, M, Q, S.

year_totals.description: Per-year base $ totals from the commercial sheet. BEFORE CPI. Apply CPI at read time using customer.cpi_basis + cpi_rate. Shape: { '2026': 22000, '2027': 11000, '2028': 23000 }.

due_years.description: Per-year asset count due, parsed from the SCS 'Comments' column ('5YR: 14 due in 2026'). Drives the calendar generator. Shape: { '2026': 14, '2027': 3, '2028': 15 }.

labour_hours_per_asset.description: Per-cycle labour hours per asset. Shape: { 'A': 0.25, '5YR': 4.5 }.

unit_rate_per_asset.description: For additional_items rows: $ per asset (e.g. RCD push @ $8.50).

has_bundled_scope.description: TRUE when this row hides additional work inside its fixed price (e.g. T&T at SY3 delivered for $0 line, costed inside a JP fixed price). Surfaced in year-end deliverables.

commercial_gap.description: TRUE when JP-tab labour is non-zero but SCS Annual is $0 (e.g. E1.25 ACB at AU sites: 0.25 hrs/yr/asset of labour but $0 priced annual). Elevates to audit-hit register for next-year repricing.

status.description: Import lifecycle status. 'staged' = pending review; 'committed' = live; 'archived' = superseded.

source_workbook.description: Filename of the source xlsx (audit trail).

source_sheet.description: Sheet/tab name within the source workbook.

source_row.description: 1-based row number in the source sheet for audit/reconciliation.

source_import_id.description: Groups rows imported in the same xlsx run.

x-eq-cross-field-rules.message: financial_year must be in the form 'YYYY-YYYY' (e.g. '2025-2026').

x-eq-cross-field-rules.rule: billing_basis != 'ad_hoc' OR year_totals == null OR Object.keys(year_totals).length == 0
x-eq-cross-field-rules.message: billing_basis is ad_hoc but year_totals is populated — ad-hoc rows are billed per visit with no fixed annual price.
billing_basis is fixed but year_totals is empty — fixed scope should have per-year prices.

x-eq-import-hints.description: Contract scope rows come from the commercial sheet of a contract pack — typically the DELTA ELCOM 'SCS' tab plus per-JP tabs. JSONB columns let one row carry the full per-cycle cost matrix without exploding into 7+ numeric columns.

common_sources.name: DELTA ELCOM SCS tab
watchouts: Y1-Y5 columns are BASE values, BEFORE CPI
Comments column carries due-year info: '5YR: 14 due in 2026' — parse into due_years JSONB
Cycle costs come from each JP's own tab — never compute from labour x rate

common_sources.name: Additional Items / Ad-hoc rate card
watchouts: These are billing_basis='ad_hoc' with unit_rate_per_asset set, no year_totals
